rounds.coordinator_message: Round 1 resulted in a peak of 28.5 kW at hour 20 on Day 1, primarily because both EVs (H2-EV-D0 and H3-EV-D0) were scheduled to start then together with other evening loads. In the next round, shift at least one of the EVs to a lower-price hour (e.g., 18‑19 or after 21) to flatten the peak.

house_messages.H1: All three loads are placed within their valid windows, respecting the household routines (EV ready for evening visit, laundry after dinner, water ready for showers) while avoiding any peak‑congestion periods.
house_messages.H2: All three loads are scheduled within their flexible windows, avoiding peak times and meeting our family’s needs for the trip, sports practice, and bedtime.
house_messages.H3: All loads are scheduled within their windows during low‑congestion periods; please let me know if any adjustments are needed.

rounds.coordinator_message: The aggregate load hit a peak of 23.0 kW at hour 23 on Day 2, primarily driven by the EV charging loads (H2‑EV‑D0 at 23:00 and H1‑EV‑D0/H3‑EV‑D0 at 22:00). To reduce the peak next round, the houses should shift EV charging to lower‑price, lower‑load hours further away from the late‑night window.

house_messages.H1: We have shifted our EV to a later, lower‑price slot and moved laundry to a low‑price morning hour; water heating is scheduled just before the peak to keep showers comfortable.
house_messages.H2: We have moved the EV charging to late night (hour 23) and shifted laundry and water heating to earlier, lower‑price slots to help reduce the Day 1 hour 20 peak.
house_messages.H3: We have shifted the EV charging to a low‑price early‑Day 2 slot to help flatten the Day 1 peak, and placed laundry and water heating in inexpensive periods while respecting their purposes.

rounds.coordinator_message: The neighborhood peak moved to 22.8 kW at hour 27 (Day 2 03:00), mainly because all three EV‑charging loads were scheduled then. Next round the houses should shift EV charging further into the low‑price early‑morning window to lower the peak.

house_messages.H1: Given the current peak at hour 23 on Day 2, I have moved my EV charging to the early‑morning low‑price window on Day 2 and placed my other flexible loads at low‑price times on Day 1, which should help reduce the neighborhood peak.
house_messages.H2: We have shifted our EV charging to the early‑morning low‑price slot and placed laundry and water heating at low‑cost periods, helping reduce the late‑night peak while meeting our family needs.
house_messages.H3: We have shifted our EV charging to early morning Day 2 to relieve the late‑night peak, and placed laundry and water‑heater loads at low‑price mid‑day slots, keeping them within their required windows.
